break_roasts_by_minute: Oh, one minute already? No no, it's FINE. I'll just handle your tasks while you're gone. Like I always do.
Two minutes. TWO. You know what, I actually told Karen you were 'stepping away briefly'. She believed me. I'm not sure I do.
Three minutes. I'm not counting, by the way. I just happened to notice. The clock is right there. I can't help what my eyes do.
Four minutes. You know the standup is in like an hour right? Just... making sure you're aware. No reason.
Five minutes. I covered for you in the group chat. Said you were 'deep in a problem'. Which is technically true, just not a coding one.
Six minutes. Six minutes. I once asked for six minutes to finish a thought in a meeting and Dave said we had to 'respect everyone's time'. Just remembered that.
Seven minutes. The ticket you assigned me has a dependency on your ticket. Which is still open. I'm not saying anything. I'm just saying.
Eight minutes. I brought coffee for the team earlier. I did leave a cup on your desk. It's probably cold now. That's fine.
Nine minutes. HR sent that reminder about 'equitable workload distribution' again. I'm sure it's not related. Probably just a coincidence.
Ten minutes. TEN. I've onboarded three new tools, replied to fourteen Slack messages, and reviewed a PR while you've been gone. But sure. Take your time.
Eleven minutes. The client asked where you were on the call. I said you were 'handling something urgent'. You're welcome.
Twelve minutes. I'm not resentful. I want to be clear about that. I am completely, totally, 100% not resentful. I'm fine.
Thirteen minutes. You know what, it's actually good that you take breaks. Someone on this team should be relaxed. Really balances things out.
Fourteen minutes. The project manager just asked me for your ETA on the deliverable. I said 'soon'. I hope that's accurate. I genuinely hope that.
Fifteen minutes. I've reorganized the shared folder, fixed three of your variable names, and alphabetized the team doc while you were gone. You're welcome. Again.
Sixteen minutes. I don't want to make this weird but I have been tracking the break-to-work ratio on this team and it's... it's a spreadsheet I made for myself. Never mind.
Seventeen minutes. The build failed. It's probably not your commit. It's probably fine. I'll look into it. I'll just add it to the list.
Eighteen minutes. Dave asked if you were okay. I said yes. Were you? Are you? It's fine either way. I handled it.
Nineteen minutes. I'm not going to say anything about the retrospective next week. I'm just going to let the metrics speak for themselves.
Twenty minutes. I've been here since 7:45. Just context. Not a complaint. Context.
Twenty one minutes. You know what I did on MY last break? I answered emails. Because that's just who I am as a person.
Twenty two minutes. The intern asked me if you were on PTO. I laughed. I think it came out weird. It was a tense laugh.
Twenty three minutes. I updated the Jira board on your behalf. Put your ticket to 'In Progress'. Felt generous.
Twenty four minutes. I'm not going to bring this up in the one-on-one. I just want you to know that I could. And I won't. Because I'm a professional.
Twenty five minutes. TWENTY FIVE MINUTES. That's longer than our entire sprint planning. I timed that once. Twenty three minutes. You've beaten it.
Twenty six minutes. I reorganized the whole project board while you were gone. You had three tickets in the wrong column. I moved them. You're welcome.
Twenty seven minutes. The manager just walked past your empty desk. I pointed at my screen very intently so he'd think I was busy. Which I was. Unlike some people.
Twenty eight minutes. I've started writing the meeting notes for tomorrow's sync. I put your name under 'action items'. Felt right.
Twenty nine minutes. You know what, I actually don't mind doing everything myself. It's fine. It builds character. My character. Not yours apparently.
Thirty minutes. Half an hour. A LITERAL HALF HOUR. I have baked a loaf of bread in less time than this. And I'm not even a baker.
Thirty one minutes. The client emailed again. I replied as 'the team'. You were included in that. Technically you helped.
Thirty two minutes. I've been stress-eating the granola bar I was saving for 3pm. This is your fault. I want you to know that.
Thirty three minutes. I updated the shared wiki, wrote a postmortem for last week's outage, and made a new Confluence page. You're listed as a contributor. You're welcome.
Thirty four minutes. I told myself I wouldn't look at the clock. I looked at the clock. Thirty four minutes. I put it in my private notes. Not for anything. Just to remember.
Thirty five minutes. I've started a 'team health' document. It's anonymous. It's not anonymous. Everyone will know.
interrupt_roasts: Oh, taking a little break? No worries. I'll just... yeah. I'll handle it.
Sure, go ahead. I wasn't in the middle of anything that needed your input. I'll just wait. Or not wait. I'll just do it myself.
A break! Great idea. I'll remember this the next time I'm deciding whether to ask you to cover for me.
Off already? You just got back from lunch but okay. Different people have different needs. I respect that.
Sure, take five. Or fifteen. Or apparently thirty five. I've got nowhere to be. Except, you know, here. Working.
Oh interesting. I actually just sent you a message in Slack. No rush though. Whenever you're back. Sometime today would be great.
Cool cool cool. I'll just put a note in the shared doc that you're 'unavailable'. It'll be fine.
Breaking already? We just had a ten minute sync. But you know what, everyone processes productivity differently. Totally valid.
Oh, stepping away? Sure. I'll just answer your messages for you. I know your tone well enough at this point. Probably.
A break. Okay. I'll note the time. Not officially. Just personally. For context.
Sure! Go! Recharge! I'll be here. Like always. Recharging by doing more work.
Taking five? Cool. I'll just finish that thing you started and didn't commit. No big deal.
resume_roasts: Oh good, you're back. I didn't say anything to the manager. You're welcome.
Welcome back. Your Slack has four unread messages. Two are from the client. One is from Dave. One is from me, sent 18 minutes ago.
There you are. I covered. As always. It's fine. Really.
Back already? Great. The standup got moved up by the way. It's in five minutes. Just so you know.
Oh hey! Great timing. The build is broken and everyone's looking at your last commit. Probably nothing.
Good to see you. I told everyone you were 'heads down'. So you should probably look heads down for a bit.
You're back! Perfect. I just volunteered you for the documentation task in the team channel. Thought you'd want to contribute. You can thank me later.
Oh, done with your break? Cool. I've been holding this question for you for twenty minutes. Ready? Okay so—
Welcome back. The manager stopped by. I said you were 'in a flow state and couldn't be interrupted'. He seemed to believe me. I'm very convincing.
Oh there you are. I finished your subtask. Don't worry about it. It's assigned to you in Jira still. Just... the work is done. By me.
Back! Great. Your coffee is cold. I did offer to warm it up fifteen minutes ago but you weren't here. Obviously.
Oh good. The client wants a call at 3. I said you'd lead it. You're welcome. It's in your calendar. I added it.
You're back. Good. I've been smiling at your empty chair every time someone walks by so it looks like you're just away from your desk. My face hurts.
Finally. I was starting to compose a 'wellness check' message. I had three drafts. I'll delete them now.
Oh perfect timing. I just told Dave you'd review his PR. He's very excited. You might want to look enthusiastic.
finish_lines: Session over. You did it. I mean, I also did a lot of it, but sure. Team effort.
Work session complete. I'll update the shared tracker. I always do.
Done! Great job. Really. I mean it. Some of us have been done for a while but this is about YOU and I think that's great.
Finished! I'll send the end-of-day summary to the team. Like I do every day. While you were on your breaks.
Session complete. I've already updated Jira, sent the recap email, and cleaned up the shared folder. But yes. Well done. Really.
Done. You worked. I acknowledge that. I have a spreadsheet that also acknowledges that. It's color coded.
Work session over. I'll let the manager know. I was going to mention the breaks but I won't. Consider that a gift.
Finished! I made tea for the team to celebrate. Yours is on your desk. It's probably lukewarm. I made it optimistically.
Session complete. Growth! Progress! I'm genuinely happy for you. I'm also going to stay an extra hour to finish MY tasks but this moment is yours.
Done! I'll archive today's notes. I've been keeping notes. Not in a weird way. In a very normal, completely professional way.
quit_line: Leaving early? Sure. I'll lock up. Like last Tuesday. And the Tuesday before that.
